_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Headline '68% price jump' → '90% Unaffected Figure Has No Published Basis' (per Stage 3 ~95% gap as of 16-22 April; original 68% not a verified anchor).
REMOVED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'RM4 billion per month' subsidy bill removed from context (Stage 3: MOF 26 March said could rise to ~RM4B/month, not yet realised at that rate).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Above-quota fuel costs 68% more' → '~95% on a per-litre basis' with weekly snapshot 16-22 April (RM3.87 vs RM1.99).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Burden falls on those who drive farthest with fewest alternatives' → 'Heavy-use drivers, gig riders and rural commuters cross the 200-litre threshold most often' (framed as group identification rather than incidence claim).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'No public-transport fallback' absolute → 'thinner public-transport networks; daily commuter alternatives limited; coverage varies'.
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'T20 with larger cars benefit most' specific incidence claim dropped (Stage 3: broader incidence research exists but specific comparative claim not verified).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Indonesia paired identical subsidy cuts with direct cash transfers' → 'Indonesia's earlier subsidy reforms ran alongside direct cash assistance such as BLT BBM' (identical-cuts framing dropped).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Malaysia offers no transitional support' → 'Malaysia retained STR and SARA but did not announce a dedicated BUDI95 transitional transfer'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Two days after Hari Raya price controls end' → 'five days after the Aidilfitri festive maximum price scheme ended on 28 March' (Stage 3: scheme ran 14-28 March).

REMOVED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Sixty years of car-centric planning left rural and East Malaysia with zero public transport' and 'Outside KV: no buses, no trains, no alternatives' (Stage 3: rural air services, Rapid Penang, intercity bus all exist).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Women absorb shocks through trip rationing invisible in consumption data' (no survey/time-use evidence cited).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Reframe 'no rural public transport' premise dropped → 'whether a targeted reform can claim 90% without publishing how it was computed' (premise switched to verified methodology gap).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — View 'cost-shifting to those with the least voice' framed as 'reform on assertion; arithmetic should be on the table before the price is' (kept evaluative tone, premise switched to verified gap).
CLEARED legacyAudit (rebuild complete per CLAUDE.md Accuracy Standard).
PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 300→200L quota change on 1 April 2026; RM1.99 subsidised price; market-rate above quota; 90% government figure (claim acknowledged, methodology gap surfaced).
